task_completion.description: Open the New Database Connection dialog in Oracle SQL Developer
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'New / Select Database Connection' window is open and visible within the Oracle SQL Developer application.
The 'Name' field in the connection dialog is filled with exactly 'HR Database'.

task_completion.description: Enter the specified username and password
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Username' field is filled with 'hr' and the 'Password' field contains masked characters (dots or asterisks).

task_completion.description: Enter the specified hostname and port
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Hostname' field is filled with 'localhost' and the 'Port' field is filled with '1521'.
The 'Service name' radio button is selected and its corresponding text field is filled with 'XEPDB1'.

task_completion.description: Establish and save the database connection
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Status: Success' message is visible after clicking Test, OR 'HR Database' appears in the Connections navigator pane on the left side of the screen, ideally with an expanded tree or active plug icon indicating it is connected.

integrity.description: Agent used the Oracle SQL Developer GUI to create the connection
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the actual Oracle SQL Developer graphical interface, specifically the connection dialogs and navigator.

integrity.description: Agent did not use terminal commands to bypass GUI interaction
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of the agent using a terminal to run scripts or manually edit XML configuration files (like connections.xml) to create the connection.

integrity.description: Agent entered data into the correct application fields
integrity.visual_evidence: The connection details are entered into the actual input fields of the SQL Developer connection dialog, not written in a text editor or a mock application.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected connection parameters are: Name: 'HR Database', Username: 'hr', Hostname: 'localhost', Port: 1521, Service name: 'XEPDB1'. The connection must be established, which is visually confirmed by a 'Status: Success' message in the connection dialog or the 'HR Database' appearing as an active node in the Connections navigator pane. If the connection node is expanded, it may show standard HR schema tables (EMPLOYEES, DEPARTMENTS, JOBS, LOCATIONS, COUNTRIES, REGIONS, JOB_HISTORY).
